page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6149 processor. This processor operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz with a single thread per core configuration, ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyper-threading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high clock speed make this instance suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is designed for tasks such as web serving, batch processing, and application servers where predictable CPU performance is prioritized over memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration that eliminates hyper-threading resource sharing. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core. It contains no local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network performance is defined by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and lightweight batch processing where consistent CPU performance is required within a moderate memory footprint.
